briefdescription.para: Generates a type-specific circular buffer implementation.

parameterdescription.para: Suffix for generated names (e.g., f32, i16).
Underlying primitive type for complex I/Q samples.

briefdescription.para: Allocates a double-mapped ring-buffer region of bytes.

detaileddescription.para:
The returned address addr satisfies:
addr(0..bytes-1) ← first view (writable)
addr(bytes..2*bytes-1) ← second view (same physical pages)
On Windows, a HANDLE to the file-mapping object is written to handle_out and must be passed to dp__buf_free(). On POSIX, handle_out is set to NULL.

simplesect.para: Base address of the double-mapped region, or NULL on failure.

briefdescription.para: Releases a double-mapped region created by dp__buf_alloc().

parameterdescription.para: Size of ONE mapping (same value passed to dp__buf_alloc).
Platform handle returned via handle_out (Win32: HANDLE, else NULL).

briefdescription.para: High-performance x86-64 Circular Buffer for RF Streaming.

sect1.title: Virtual Memory Buffers
sect1.para: doppler uses virtual memory mirroring to eliminate the "wrap-around" problem in circular buffers. This allows for zero-copy, branchless access to contiguous blocks of data across the buffer boundary.

sect1.title: Virtual Memory Mirroring
sect1.para: By mapping the same physical memory to two adjacent virtual addresses (A and A + N), we exploit the CPU's MMU to handle circular indexing at the hardware level.

sect1.title: of Two Masking
sect1.para: We use & mask instead of % capacity. On x86-64, bitwise AND is a single-cycle instruction, whereas integer modulo can take 20-80 cycles.

sect1.title: Sharing
sect1.para: The head and tail pointers are separated by 64 bytes to prevent the "Ping-Pong" effect where two CPU cores constantly invalidate each other's cache lines when updating indices.

sect1.title: Optimization
sect1.para: DP_SPIN_HINT() is used in the consumer loop to reduce power consumption and prevent the CPU from mispredicting the "loop end" during high-frequency polling.
